title: Decadent Buttery Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and savory Egyptian dish featuring lamb, black olives, and a blend of spices, all wrapped in a lotus leaf and slow-cooked to perfection, making it an ideal hangover cure due to its hearty and comforting nature.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the melted butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced black olives, ground cumin, ground coriander, and dried thyme, stirring to combine.
Cover the Dutch oven with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb mixture for 2 1/2 hours, or until it is tender and falls apart easily.
While the lamb is cooking, prepare the lotus leaves by rinsing them in cold water and patting them dry with paper towels.
To serve, place a portion of the lamb mixture onto the center of each lotus leaf, then fold the leaves over the filling to form a tight packet.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional black olives and melted butter if desired.
